pi_items.source: Successful download and inspection of the Natural Earth dataset from naciscdn.org.
Successful download and inspection of the Natural Earth dataset from naciscdn.org.
Matches task instructions and export_result.sh checks.
PROJ database query (proj.db) confirms 102033 is an ESRI code for 'South_America_Albers_Equal_Area_Conic'. EPSG:102033 does not exist.

Geopandas distance calculation using ESRI:102033 on Natural Earth data confirms Lima=6.26km, Brasilia=889.30km, Bogota=353.28km, Santiago=87.71km, La Paz=311.99km, all falling strictly within the specified min/max km bounds.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real Natural Earth GIS datasets. Programmatic verification using Geopandas confirms the baseline distances to the coastline fall neatly within the benchmark bounds (Lima: ~6.3km, Brasilia: ~889km, Bogota: ~353km, Santiago: ~88km, La Paz: ~312km). However, the metadata claim for the target projection (EPSG:102033) is a misnomer; the true PROJ authority for the South America Albers Equal Area Conic projection is ESRI:102033.
